source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.1 Electric Charges and Fields (Gauss's law statement §1.18) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (the prerequisite electric_flux ended at 'net flux through a closed surface is proportional to the charge inside' → name the exact constant of proportionality: it is 1/ε₀, so net Φ = q_enc/ε₀ → ε₀ is one fixed constant of nature, 8.854×10⁻¹² → because the net depends only on q_enc, ANY closed surface you imagine around the charge — sphere, cube, blob — gives the same q_enc/ε₀, and we call that imagined surface a Gaussian surface → a charge sitting OUTSIDE any closed surface contributes exactly zero, since every line that enters also leaves → and q_enc is the ALGEBRAIC sum of the enclosed charges, signs and all, so the net flux can be zero or negative → finally let the student add, remove and reshape and watch the live Φ = q_enc/ε₀ readout follow only the enclosed charge), never copied. The atomic idea: the net electric flux through ANY closed surface equals the charge enclosed divided by the permittivity of free space, Φ = q_enc/ε₀, with ε₀ = 8.854×10⁻¹² C²/(N·m²) a fixed constant of nature; the value is set ONLY by q_enc and is utterly independent of the surface's shape or size (any such imagined closed surface is a Gaussian surface); a charge outside contributes exactly zero; and q_enc is the signed algebraic sum of the enclosed charges, so net flux can be zero or negative. SCOPE: STATEMENT ONLY — no E-from-symmetry derivation (that is deferred to gauss_law_sphere etc.). Reuses the field_3d closed-surface machinery from electric_flux but flips the net-flux LABEL from '∝ q_enc' to '= q_enc/ε₀' and writes ε₀ on-canvas from STATE_2 — this INTENTIONALLY inverts electric_flux's 'no ε₀' cut-line guard (architect FLAG #1). Prerequisites electric_flux + charge_distribution. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: Light some agarbatti — incense sticks — inside a closed room. The total amount of smoke leaving the room each second depends only on how many sticks are burning INSIDE, not on the shape of the room and not on a stick burning in the neighbour's room next door. Draw any imaginary bubble around the burning sticks — a sphere, a box, any closed shape — and the same total smoke crosses out of it. That is Gauss's law: the net flux out of any closed surface is set only by the source enclosed, and the electric version fixes the exact amount as q_enc divided by ε₀.
real_world_anchor.secondary: The charged metal dome of a Van de Graaff generator in the school lab carries a fixed charge. Imagine any closed bubble drawn around the dome — close to it or far, round or lumpy — and the net electric field crossing out of that bubble is the same, fixed entirely by the charge on the dome and the constant ε₀, never by the bubble you chose to draw.

q_enc.name: Charge enclosed by the Gaussian surface

charge_x.name: Position of the enclosed charge inside the surface

epsilon_0.name: Permittivity of free space (a fixed constant of nature)

DEMO_FLUX_PER_NC.name: Demo flux readout per nanocoulomb enclosed

formulas.gauss_law: Φ = q_enc / ε₀   (net flux through any closed surface)
formulas.epsilon_value: ε₀ = 8.854×10⁻¹² C²/(N·m²)   (one fixed constant of nature)
formulas.shape_independence: any closed surface around the same q_enc gives the same Φ = q_enc/ε₀ — sphere, cube or blob
formulas.enclosed_only: only the charge INSIDE the closed surface sets the net flux
formulas.outside_zero: a charge outside a closed surface ⇒ net Φ = 0 (every line entering also leaves)
formulas.algebraic_sum: q_enc = Σ qᵢ   (the signed algebraic sum of the enclosed charges)
formulas.sign_of_flux: net Φ > 0 for net + charge inside, net Φ < 0 for net − charge inside, 0 when they cancel

constraints: The net flux through a closed surface is computed for the surface as a WHOLE, not face by face — so the surface's shape never changes it; only the enclosed charge does (M1).
ε₀ is a single fixed constant of nature, 8.854×10⁻¹² C²/(N·m²); it does not depend on the surface, the medium in this vacuum statement, or any choice you make (M2).
A bigger or lumpier closed surface does NOT catch more or less net flux — any closed surface around the same q_enc gives exactly q_enc/ε₀ (M3).
A charge sitting OUTSIDE the closed surface contributes exactly zero net flux, no matter how close it is — every field line that enters one part of the surface leaves through another (M4).
q_enc is the signed ALGEBRAIC sum of the enclosed charges, Σ qᵢ — so the net flux can be zero (equal + and −) or negative (net − charge inside), not just a positive total (M5).
A Gaussian surface is an IMAGINARY closed surface you choose for convenience; it is not a real object and need not coincide with any physical boundary.

aha_moment.statement: Net flux through any closed surface is the same: set only by the charge inside.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The closed surface morphs from a sphere to a cube to a lumpy blob while the net-flux readout stays pinned at exactly q_enc/ε₀ the whole time.

STATE_1.title: Net flux is set by the charge inside — but by exactly how much?

scene_composition.text: net Φ depends only on q_enc
net Φ ∝ q_enc   (the constant comes next)

tts_sentences.text_en: From electric flux you know the net flux out of any closed surface is set only by the charge trapped inside it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Electric flux से आप जानते हैं कि किसी भी closed surface से निकलने वाला net flux केवल उसके अंदर trapped charge से set होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Electric flux నుండి మీకు తెలుసు, ఏ closed surface నుండి అయినా బయటకి వచ్చే net flux దాని లోపల trapped అయిన charge ద్వారా మాత్రమే set అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: So far we only said net flux is proportional to the enclosed charge — without yet naming the exact constant that multiplies it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तक हमने सिर्फ कहा था कि net flux, enclosed charge के proportional है — लेकिन उस exact constant का नाम अभी नहीं लिया जो इसे multiply करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పటివరకు మేము కేవలం net flux, enclosed charge కి proportional అని మాత్రమే చెప్పాము — దాన్ని multiply చేసే exact constant పేరు ఇంకా చెప్పలేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The net flux is not simply equal to the charge inside — one fixed number of nature multiplies it, and that constant comes next.
tts_sentences.text_hi: net flux सिर्फ अंदर के charge के equal नहीं है — प्रकृति का एक fixed number इसे multiply करता है, और वह constant आगे आता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: net flux కేవలం లోపల ఉన్న charge కి equal కాదు — ప్రకృతి యొక్క ఒక fixed number దాన్ని multiply చేస్తుంది, ఆ constant తరువాత వస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The net flux is computed face by face, so the surface's shape changes how much you get.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Coming up, the very same charge inside a sphere and inside a lumpy blob gives the identical net flux q_enc/ε₀.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Net flux is fixed by the enclosed charge alone; the surface shape is your free choice.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is the answer. The proportional sign becomes an equals, and the exact constant multiplying the charge is one over epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ इसका उत्तर है। Proportional sign, equals बन जाता है, और charge को multiply करने वाला exact constant है one over epsilon-nought।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ సమాధానం ఉంది. Proportional sign, equals అవుతుంది, మరియు charge ని multiply చేసే exact constant, one over epsilon-nought.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the net flux equals the enclosed charge divided by epsilon-nought. This is Gauss's law: Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो net flux, enclosed charge divided by epsilon-nought के equal है। यह Gauss's law है: Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి net flux, enclosed charge divided by epsilon-nought కి equal అవుతుంది. ఇది Gauss's law: Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought.

tts_sentences.text_en: Epsilon-nought is a fixed constant of nature, eight point eight five times ten to the minus twelve. Same everywhere, never a fudge factor.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Epsilon-nought प्रकृति का एक fixed constant है, eight point eight five times ten to the minus twelve। यह हर जगह same रहता है, कभी fudge factor नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Epsilon-nought ప్రకృతి యొక్క fixed constant, eight point eight five times ten to the minus twelve. ఇది ఎక్కడైనా same, ఎప్పుడూ fudge factor కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: ε₀ is some adjustable factor that depends on the surface, the medium, or is just a fudge to make the numbers fit.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The fixed value 8.854×10⁻¹² writes onto the canvas and stays put no matter what surface or charge is shown.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: ε₀ is one fixed constant of nature, the same for every closed surface.

scene_composition.text: Gaussian surface = any closed surface you choose

tts_sentences.text_en: Any closed surface you imagine around the charge is called a Gaussian surface. It is imaginary — you choose it for convenience.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge के चारों ओर आप जिस भी closed surface की कल्पना करते हैं, उसे Gaussian surface कहते हैं। यह imaginary है — आप इसे convenience के लिए चुनते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge చుట్టూ మీరు ఊహించుకునే ఏ closed surface నైనా Gaussian surface అంటారు. ఇది imaginary — మీరు దీన్ని convenience కోసం ఎంచుకుంటారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now I start changing the surface, from a small sphere to a bigger one, then a lumpier shape.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब मैं surface को बदलना शुरू करता हूँ — एक small sphere से एक bigger shape में, फिर एक lumpier shape में।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు నేను surface ని మార్చడం మొదలుపెడతాను — ఒక small sphere నుండి ఒక bigger shape కి, ఆపై ఒక lumpier shape కి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch: the surface morphs from a sphere to a cube to a lumpy blob, and the net-flux readout never moves off q-enclosed over epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए: surface एक sphere से cube में, फिर एक lumpy blob में बदल जाती है, और net-flux readout कभी भी q-enclosed over epsilon-nought से नहीं हटता।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి: surface ఒక sphere నుండి cube కి, ఆపై lumpy blob కి మారుతుంది, మరియు net-flux readout ఎప్పుడూ q-enclosed over epsilon-nought నుండి మారదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is the whole point. Any closed surface around the same charge gives the same Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही पूरा point है। Same charge के चारों ओर कोई भी closed surface वही Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే ముఖ్యం. Same charge చుట్టూ ఉన్న ఏ closed surface అయినా అదే Phi equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought ని ఇస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A bigger or lumpier closed surface catches more, or less, net flux than a small neat one.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The surface morphs sphere → cube → blob while the readout holds dead steady at q_enc/ε₀.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Any closed surface around the same q_enc gives the same q_enc/ε₀ — shape and size never matter.

scene_composition.text: this charge sits OUTSIDE the surface
every line in also leaves → net 0

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the charge sits entirely outside the closed surface. Its field still reaches the surface, yet the net flux is about to come out zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब charge पूरी तरह से closed surface के बाहर है। इसका field फिर भी surface तक पहुँचता है, फिर भी net flux zero निकलने वाला है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు charge పూర్తిగా closed surface బయట ఉంది. దీని field ఇంకా surface కు చేరుతుంది, అయినా net flux zero గా రాబోతోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the lines. The field enters the surface on one side and leaves on the other — every line in also comes out.
tts_sentences.text_hi: lines को देखिए। field एक तरफ से surface में enter करता है और दूसरी तरफ से leave करता है — हर incoming line बाहर भी आती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: lines ని చూడండి. field ఒక వైపు నుండి surface లోకి enter అవుతుంది మరియు మరొక వైపు నుండి leave అవుతుంది — లోపలికి వచ్చే ప్రతి line బయటకు కూడా వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the plus and minus contributions cancel exactly. The net flux drops to zero, no matter how close the outside charge gets.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो plus और minus contributions बिल्कुल cancel हो जाते हैं। net flux zero हो जाता है, चाहे outside charge कितना भी पास क्यों न हो।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి plus మరియు minus contributions ఖచ్చితంగా cancel అవుతాయి. net flux zero కి పడిపోతుంది, outside charge ఎంత దగ్గరగా ఉన్నా సరే.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is why only the enclosed charge counts in Gauss's law. Outside charges contribute exactly zero net flux.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसीलिए Gauss's law में सिर्फ enclosed charge ही गिना जाता है। Outside charges बिल्कुल zero net flux contribute करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: అందుకే Gauss's law లో enclosed charge మాత్రమే లెక్కించబడుతుంది. Outside charges ఖచ్చితంగా zero net flux ని contribute చేస్తాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: A charge just outside the surface still pushes some net flux through it, and the closer it is, the more it adds.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: As the outside charge approaches, the field lines that enter one side leave the other; the readout holds at exactly zero.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A charge outside a closed surface contributes exactly zero net flux — every line that enters also leaves.

STATE_5.title: q_enc is the ALGEBRAIC sum — signs matter

scene_composition.text: net Φ can be 0, or negative

tts_sentences.text_en: Now put two charges inside, a plus charge q and a minus charge q, and find the enclosed charge that goes into Gauss's law.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दो charges को अंदर रखिए, एक plus charge q और एक minus charge q, और Gauss's law में जाने वाला enclosed charge निकालिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు charges ని లోపల ఉంచండి, ఒక plus charge q మరియు ఒక minus charge q, మరియు Gauss's law లోకి వెళ్ళే enclosed charge ని కనుక్కోండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It is the algebraic sum, q-enclosed equals the sum of the charges with their signs. Plus charge q and minus charge q add to zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह algebraic sum है, q-enclosed equals the sum of the charges with their signs। Plus charge q और minus charge q add होकर zero बनते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది algebraic sum, q-enclosed equals the sum of the charges with their signs. Plus charge q మరియు minus charge q కలిపి zero అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the net flux is zero, even though there is charge inside. Now I add a second negative charge inside the same surface.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो net flux zero है, भले ही अंदर charge हो। अब मैं उसी surface के अंदर एक second negative charge डालता हूँ।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి net flux zero, లోపల charge ఉన్నప్పటికీ. ఇప్పుడు నేను అదే surface లోపల ఒక second negative charge ని add చేస్తాను.

tts_sentences.text_en: The enclosed sum is now negative, so the readout turns red and the field points inward. q-enclosed is a signed sum.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब enclosed sum negative है, इसलिए readout red हो जाता है और field inward point करता है। q-enclosed एक signed sum है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు enclosed sum negative, కాబట్టి readout red రంగులోకి మారుతుంది మరియు field inward గా point చేస్తుంది. q-enclosed అనేది signed sum.

misconception_watch.belief: q_enc is the total or the biggest charge inside, and flux can never be negative.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: +q and −q give net zero; adding a second negative charge drives q_enc negative, so the flux turns red and points inward.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: q_enc is the signed algebraic sum Σ qᵢ; net flux can be zero or negative.

STATE_6.title: Explore: add, remove and reshape — watch Φ = q_enc/ε₀

scene_composition.text: only q_enc moves the readout — never the shape

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Use the sliders to add or remove enclosed charge and to reshape the closed surface.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप इसे चलाइए। enclosed charge को add या remove करने के लिए और closed surface को reshape करने के लिए sliders का उपयोग करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు దీన్ని నడపండి. enclosed charge ని add లేదా remove చేయడానికి మరియు closed surface ని reshape చేయడానికి sliders ని ఉపయోగించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the live readout. It always equals q-enclosed over epsilon-nought, following only the charge you trap inside.
tts_sentences.text_hi: live readout को देखिए। यह हमेशा q-enclosed over epsilon-nought के बराबर होता है, केवल उस charge को follow करते हुए जिसे आप अंदर trap करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: live readout ని చూడండి. ఇది మీరు లోపల trap చేసిన charge ని మాత్రమే అనుసరించి, ఎల్లప్పుడూ q-enclosed over epsilon-nought కి సమానంగా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Reshape the surface all you like — the readout will not budge unless you change the enclosed charge. That is Gauss's law.
tts_sentences.text_hi: surface को आप जितना चाहें reshape कर लें — readout बिल्कुल नहीं हिलेगा जब तक आप enclosed charge को नहीं बदलते। यही Gauss's law है।
tts_sentences.text_te: surface ని మీకు నచ్చినంత reshape చేయండి — readout అస్సలు కదలదు మీరు enclosed charge ను మార్చకపోతే తప్ప. అది Gauss's law.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the statement of Gauss's law when they can (1) state that the net electric flux through any closed surface equals the enclosed charge divided by ε₀, Φ = q_enc/ε₀; (2) say that ε₀ = 8.854×10⁻¹² C²/(N·m²) is a fixed constant of nature, not a surface- or medium-dependent factor; (3) explain that the net flux is set ONLY by q_enc and is independent of the closed surface's shape and size — that any such imagined closed surface is a Gaussian surface; (4) state that a charge entirely outside a closed surface contributes exactly zero net flux because every line that enters also leaves; and (5) treat q_enc as the signed algebraic sum Σ qᵢ of the enclosed charges, so that the net flux can be zero (equal + and −) or negative (net − charge inside).

questions.stem: A single charge q sits inside a closed surface. According to Gauss's law, what is the net electric flux through the surface?

options.A: q/ε₀ — the enclosed charge divided by the permittivity of free space
options.B: q — the net flux is simply equal to the enclosed charge
options.C: q·ε₀ — the enclosed charge times the permittivity of free space
options.D: ε₀/q — the permittivity divided by the enclosed charge

distractor_misconceptions.B: drops the constant ε₀ entirely, leaving net flux equal to the charge
distractor_misconceptions.C: multiplies by ε₀ instead of dividing by it
distractor_misconceptions.D: inverts the relation, dividing ε₀ by the charge

questions.tested_idea: Gauss's law: the net flux through a closed surface is q_enc/ε₀.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A burning agarbatti stick sits inside a sealed room. If the room's net 'smoke flux' follows the same rule, write the net flux in terms of the enclosed source and the constant ε₀.

questions.stem: The same charge q is placed first inside a small sphere, then inside a large cube drawn around it. How does the net flux through the cube compare with the net flux through the sphere?

options.A: It is the same — both equal q/ε₀, since net flux depends only on the enclosed charge, not the surface's shape or size
options.B: It is larger for the cube, because the cube has more surface area to let flux through
options.C: It is smaller for the cube, because the flux spreads out over a bigger surface
options.D: It cannot be compared — the flux through a cube and a sphere are computed by different rules

distractor_misconceptions.B: thinks a bigger surface catches more net flux
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks a bigger surface dilutes the net flux
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks surface shape changes which rule applies

questions.tested_idea: Net flux is independent of the closed surface's shape and size; any surface around q gives q/ε₀.
questions.parallel_form_stem: One burning agarbatti stick sits inside a small round paper lantern, then inside a large box-shaped lantern. Does the total smoke leaving change when you swap the lantern's shape, and why?

questions.stem: A point charge sits entirely OUTSIDE a closed surface; no charge is inside. What is the net electric flux through that surface?

options.A: Zero — every field line that enters the surface on one side leaves on another, so the contributions cancel
options.B: Positive — the outside charge's field still points through the surface, so it pushes a net flux out
options.C: It depends on the distance — the closer the outside charge, the larger the net flux through the surface
options.D: q_out/ε₀ — the outside charge counts in Gauss's law just like an inside one

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes the outside field always yields a positive net flux
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks the net flux grows as the outside charge gets nearer
distractor_misconceptions.D: treats an outside charge as if it were enclosed

questions.tested_idea: A charge outside a closed surface contributes exactly zero net flux.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Your neighbour burns an agarbatti stick in the next room, with none in yours. What is the net smoke flux out of your sealed room, and why?

questions.stem: A closed surface encloses a charge of +2 nC and a charge of −2 nC. What is the net electric flux through the surface?

options.A: Zero — q_enc is the signed algebraic sum, +2 + (−2) = 0, so the net flux is zero
options.B: 4 nC/ε₀ — add up the magnitudes of all the charges inside
options.C: 2 nC/ε₀ — use the larger of the two enclosed charges
options.D: Net flux can never be zero when there is charge inside the surface

distractor_misconceptions.B: sums charge magnitudes instead of the signed algebraic sum
distractor_misconceptions.C: uses the biggest single charge rather than the algebraic sum
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes net flux must be nonzero whenever charge is present

questions.tested_idea: q_enc is the signed algebraic sum Σ qᵢ, so equal + and − charges give zero net flux.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A sealed box on the Van de Graaff bench encloses one +2 nC clip and one −2 nC clip. Using q_enc = Σ qᵢ, what is the net flux through the box?

questions.stem: In Gauss's law, what exactly is a 'Gaussian surface'?

options.A: Any imaginary closed surface you choose around the charge for convenience — it need not be a real physical object
options.B: A real metal surface that must physically surround the charge for the law to hold
options.C: Only a perfect sphere centred on the charge — no other shape is allowed
options.D: The actual outer surface of the charged body itself

distractor_misconceptions.B: thinks the Gaussian surface must be a real material boundary
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks only a centred sphere qualifies as a Gaussian surface
distractor_misconceptions.D: confuses the imagined surface with the charged body's own surface

questions.tested_idea: A Gaussian surface is an imaginary closed surface chosen for convenience — any closed shape works.
questions.parallel_form_stem: When we say 'draw any bubble around the burning agarbatti', what kind of bubble counts — a real wall, or any imagined closed shape? Tie this to the term Gaussian surface.

questions.stem: A closed surface encloses a charge of +2q. A separate charge of −q sits just outside the surface. What is the net electric flux through the surface?

options.A: 2q/ε₀ — only the enclosed +2q counts; the outside −q contributes exactly zero
options.B: q/ε₀ — combine the inside +2q and the outside −q to get a net q
options.C: 3q/ε₀ — add the magnitudes of the inside +2q and the outside −q
options.D: Zero — the outside −q cancels part of the inside charge's flux

distractor_misconceptions.B: lets the outside charge combine into q_enc
distractor_misconceptions.C: sums all charge magnitudes regardless of inside/outside
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks the outside charge cancels enclosed flux

questions.tested_idea: Only enclosed charge sets the net flux: q_enc = +2q gives 2q/ε₀; an outside charge contributes zero.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two agarbatti sticks burn inside your sealed room and one burns just outside the door. Counting only what is enclosed, what is the net smoke flux out of your room?

STATE_1.label: Net flux is set by the charge inside — but by exactly how much?

STATE_3.label: Any closed (Gaussian) surface — same q_enc/ε₀ (PRIMARY aha)

STATE_5.label: q_enc is the ALGEBRAIC sum — signs matter

STATE_6.label: Explore: add, remove and reshape — watch Φ = q_enc/ε₀
